steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank connects two sentences: JWST is almost one million miles beyond Earth’s orbit; Hubble is only about 340 miles above Earth’s surface. The word ‘only’ before ‘340 miles’ signals that the second sentence is drawing a stark contrast with the first. The transition must mark opposition.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Therefore,": Signals a conclusion or effect caused by the previous sentence. Hubble’s location is not a consequence of JWST’s location. ✗
  • "By contrast,": Signals that the following information opposes what came before — fitting here because Hubble’s 340-mile orbit is being set against JWST’s one-million-mile position. ✓
  • "Similarly,": Signals likeness or agreement. The two telescopes’ positions are drastically different, not similar. ✗
  • "Secondly,": Signals an item in a list. The passage is contrasting two specific facts, not listing sequential points. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
